key_assumptions.input: Starting free cash flow per share
key_assumptions.source: FY2025 operating income $3.44B plus normalized net investment income contribution, ÷ 534.9M shares = $5.85/sh
Low-single-digit premium growth in Japan and U.S. worksite channel
Four proof-backed additive components in valuation.json

open_questions: Segment owner-cash split uses earned premium proportion pending segment operating income disclosure
Investment marks moved GAAP net income to $3.65B from $5.44B in 2024
Yen translation and JGB credit spread remain widest judgment bands
No approved third-party sources in base IRR
thesis: Aflac sells supplemental health and life insurance in Japan (cancer and medical riders) and the U.S. (worksite accident and hospital indemnity). FY2025 revenues were **$18.93B**; normalized owner cash is **~$5.85/sh** between operating cash flow and operating income. Japan earned premium **~$10.7B** (~61% of segment premiums) anchors the franchise; investment income **$4.08B** moves GAAP earnings quarter to quarter.
why_market_wrong: No dated predictive attribute. The market may treat FY2025 GAAP earnings decline (net income **$3.65B** vs **$5.44B** in 2024) as franchise impairment when much of the swing is investment marks, not lost supplemental demand. At **~$122**, return is normalized owner cash at price, not a timed mispricing catalyst.
